message:
🌱 caddy: No matching certificate to load: decoding certificate metadata: invalid (#1928)

* 🌱 Add caddy: No matching certificate to load: decoding certificate mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* 🌱 Add caddy: No matching certificate to load: decoding certificate mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix caddy-6481 mission: remove wrong PR ref, fix IP leak, correct resolution

- Remove incorrect PR #6606 (metrics, not cert metadata) from sourceUrls
- Replace real IP 77.141.97.142 with RFC 5737 documentation IP 192.0.2.1
- Replace resolution text with accurate cert metadata corruption description
- Replace steps with proper cert metadata troubleshooting workflow

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "caddy-6481-no-matching-certificate-to-load-decoding-certificate-metadata-invalid",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "caddy: No matching certificate to load: decoding certificate metadata: invalid character '}'",
+    "description": "No matching certificate to load: decoding certificate metadata: invalid character '}'. This issue affects 8+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify the corrupt certificate metadata error",
+        "description": "Check Caddy logs for the certificate metadata decoding error:\n```\ncaddy[1208]: {\"level\":\"error\",\"logger\":\"tls.on_demand\",\"msg\":\"loading newly-obtained certificate from storage\",\"remote_ip\":\"192.0.2.1\",\"remote_port\":\"49984\",\"server_name\":\"server.example.com\",\"error\":\"no matching certificate to load for server.example.com: decoding certificate metadata: invalid character '}' after top-level value\"}\n```\nThis error indicates a corrupt JSON metadata file in Caddy's certificate storage directory."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Locate the corrupt certificate metadata file",
+        "description": "Find the certificate metadata files in Caddy's data directory. The default location is `~/.local/share/caddy/certificates/` or the path configured via `storage file_system { root ... }`:\n```bash\nfind /var/lib/caddy/.local/share/caddy/certificates -name '*.json' \\\n  -exec sh -c 'python3 -m json.tool \"$1\" > /dev/null 2>&1 || echo \"CORRUPT: $1\"' _ {} \\;\n```\nThis scans all certificate metadata JSON files and reports any that fail to parse."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Delete the corrupt metadata file and regenerate",
+        "description": "Remove the corrupt metadata file for the affected domain. Caddy will automatically re-obtain the certificate and regenerate valid metadata on the next TLS handshake:\n```bash\n# Back up the corrupt file first\ncp /path/to/corrupt-metadata.json /tmp/corrupt-metadata.json.bak\n# Remove the corrupt metadata\nrm /path/to/corrupt-metadata.json\n# Restart Caddy to trigger certificate re-obtainment\nsystemctl restart caddy\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify certificates are loading correctly",
+        "description": "After restarting Caddy, verify that the certificate is obtained and loaded without errors:\n```bash\n# Check Caddy logs for successful certificate obtainment\njournalctl -u caddy --since '5 minutes ago' | grep -i certificate\n# Test TLS connection to the affected domain\ncurl -vI https://server.example.com 2>&1 | grep 'SSL certificate'\n```\nConfirm the decoding error no longer appears in the logs."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The certificate metadata corruption (invalid JSON character '}') was caused by a corrupt or hand-edited certificate metadata file in Caddy's data directory. The fix in Caddy v2.9.0 improved JSON parsing resilience for certificate metadata files. Workaround: delete the corrupt metadata file and let Caddy regenerate certificates.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "# Example corrupt metadata file (note the extra '}' at the end)\n{\n  \"sans\": [\"server.example.com\"],\n  \"issuer_data\": {\n    \"url\": \"https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/acme/cert/...\",\n    \"ca\": \"https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory\"\n  }\n}}",
+        "# Find and remove corrupt metadata files\nfind /var/lib/caddy/.local/share/caddy/certificates -name '*.json' \\\n  -exec sh -c 'python3 -m json.tool \"$1\" > /dev/null 2>&1 || echo \"CORRUPT: $1\"' _ {} \\;\n# Then delete the corrupt file and restart Caddy\nsystemctl restart caddy"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "caddy",
+      "community",
+      "web-server",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "caddy"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "advanced",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/caddyserver/caddy/issues/6481",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/caddyserver/caddy"
+    },
+    "reactions": 8,
+    "comments": 32,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "tools": [
+      "caddy"
+    ],
+    "description": "A working caddy installation or development environment."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-04-02T06:58:18.127Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}

+      ]
+    }
